term: You've Got to Be Kidding Me
englishDefinitions: An exclamation used when something seems unbelievable, shocking, or absurd.
A way to express strong surprise or disbelief.
examples: You've got to be kidding me—another delay?
When he saw the bill, he said, 'You've got to be kidding me.'
You've got to be kidding me if you think that's a good idea.
